subheading: Run Z-Image, Alibaba Tongyi Lab's open image model known for fast generation and accurate text rendering, with ComfyUI workflows on Comfy Cloud or your own GPU.
extendedDescription:
Two things stand out when you reach for Z-Image comfyui workflows: it is fast, and it actually renders text. Z-Image is Alibaba Tongyi Lab's open image-generation family, built on an S3-DiT (Scalable Single-Stream DiT) architecture that feeds text, visual-semantic, and image tokens through one unified stream. Its standout trait is accurate lettering in both English and Chinese, the kind most image models still fumble on posters and infographics. The workflows on this page are the ready-made graphs for running it without wiring the nodes yourself.

The variant most people start with is Z-Image-Turbo, a distilled 6B model that generates in roughly eight sampling steps. That is why it shows up in the quick text-to-image and 2K upscaling workflows here: it iterates fast enough to keep a tight feedback loop, and it stays under about 16GB of VRAM, so a single consumer card can run it. The weights are open on HuggingFace under an Apache 2.0 license, which is what lets you self-host instead of renting generations by the image.

What that means in practice is a model you can lean on for design work, not just pretty pictures. Generate a poster with legible headline text, create social graphics with accurate lettering, run text-to-image at speed, or push a result to a crisp 2K. Because these are open workflows, you control the resolution, the step count, and the variant you load, and you can re-run until the text and composition are right.

Everything here is open and editable. Open a Z-Image workflow on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install, or download it and run it locally if you have a capable GPU. You can swap variants, tune the parameters, and there is no watermark on what you make.

howToUse: Pick a Z-Image workflow: text-to-image, poster or graphic, or 2K upscale.
Open it on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Load the Z-Image-Turbo weights, or another variant if the workflow specifies one.
Write your prompt, including any headline text you want rendered.
Set the resolution and the step count, then run and check the text and composition.
Re-run or upscale the result, then download it with no watermark.

faqItems.question: What are Z-Image ComfyUI workflows?
faqItems.answer: They are ready-made ComfyUI graphs that run Z-Image, the open image model from Alibaba Tongyi Lab built on an S3-DiT architecture. Each wires up a task like text-to-image or 2K upscaling, so you get Z-Image's fast generation and accurate text rendering without building the graph yourself.

faqItems.question: Is Z-Image open, and who makes it?
faqItems.answer: Z-Image is made by Alibaba's Tongyi Lab, and the weights are open on HuggingFace under an Apache 2.0 license. That permissive license is why you can self-host it or run it on Comfy Cloud, with no watermark on the output.

faqItems.question: What makes Z-Image good at rendering text?
faqItems.answer: Z-Image was trained with text rendering as a priority, so it produces legible, correctly spelled lettering in both English and Chinese far more reliably than typical image models. That makes it a strong fit for posters, infographics, and social graphics where the words have to be right.

faqItems.question: How fast is Z-Image-Turbo?
faqItems.answer: Z-Image-Turbo is a distilled 6B variant that generates in roughly eight sampling steps, so it is the one to pick when you want quick iteration or many images. The shorter step count is what keeps it fast enough for a tight prompt-and-review loop.

faqItems.question: Can I run Z-Image without a powerful GPU?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Run any Z-Image workflow on Comfy Cloud in the browser with nothing to install. If you want to self-host, Z-Image-Turbo stays under about 16GB of VRAM, so a single capable consumer card can run it locally.

faqItems.question: What can I create with Z-Image workflows?
faqItems.answer: These workflows cover text-to-image generation, posters and social graphics with accurate lettering, and upscaling a result to a crisp 2K. Because they are open and editable, you can adjust the resolution and steps and re-run until the composition and text are right.

faqItems.question: What VRAM do I need to run Z-Image locally?
faqItems.answer: Z-Image-Turbo in BF16 precision wants roughly 14 to 16GB of VRAM, so a card like an RTX 4070 Ti or 4090 handles it comfortably. Quantized GGUF and FP8 builds drop the requirement to about 6 to 8GB, which brings smaller cards into range. If your GPU falls short, run the same workflow on Comfy Cloud instead.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything to use these workflows?
faqItems.answer: No. Comfy Cloud runs every Z-Image workflow in the browser with nothing to download or set up, including the model weights. If you prefer to self-host, you install ComfyUI locally and fetch the Z-Image-Turbo checkpoint once, then the workflow runs on your own GPU.

faqItems.question: Does Z-Image run locally or only on Comfy Cloud?
faqItems.answer: Both paths work, since the weights are open under Apache 2.0. You can self-host on a 16GB consumer GPU for full local control, or open the workflow on Comfy Cloud, a paid plan, when you want browser access without the hardware. The graph is identical either way.
